title: polemonium caeruleum var. lacteum
intro: Polemonium caeruleum var. lacteum, also known as milkwort or white Jacob's ladder, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the body, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Polemonium caeruleum var. lacteum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of applications in traditional folk medicine.
